text: Marcus runs a car wash business. He charges £8.50 per car for a basic wash. On any car where the customer requests a premium polish, he charges an extra 40% on top of the basic wash price. One weekend, Marcus washed 12 cars in total. 25% of those cars had the premium polish. How much money did Marcus earn that weekend?
generatorSolution:
Step 1: Find how many cars had the premium polish. 25% of 12 = 12 ÷ 4 = 3 cars.

Step 2: Find how many cars had just the basic wash. 12 − 3 = 9 cars.

Step 3: Calculate earnings from basic wash cars. 9 × £8.50 = £76.50.

Step 4: Find the price of a premium polish car. 40% of £8.50 = 0.4 × £8.50 = £3.40. So premium price = £8.50 + £3.40 = £11.90.

Step 5: Calculate earnings from premium polish cars. 3 × £11.90 = £35.70.

Step 6: Find total earnings. £76.50 + £35.70 = £112.20.

The correct answer is £112.20.
